news_title: NCC recalls over 6,500 Toyota and Lexus vehicles over rear camera fault - IOL
efet_title: NCC recalls over 6,500 Toyota and Lexus vehicles over rear camera fault - IOL
efet_body:
News
South Africa
NCC recalls over 6,500 Toyota and Lexus vehicles over rear camera fault
Robin-Lee Francke
|
Updated
1 month ago
Ask AI
1
min
The Toyota Land Cruiser 300 is among the vehicles affected.
Image:
File
The National Consumer Commission (NCC) has confirmed the recall of over 6,500 Toyota and Lexus vehicles, citing safety concerns.
The NCC said the recalls were issued by Toyota South Africa Motors (TSAM) and Lexus South Africa Motors.
According to the NCC’s spokesperson, Pheto Ntaba, the recall affects 4,858 Toyota vehicles and 1,667 Lexus vehicles sold across South Africa.
The affected Toyota models include the Toyota Crown, Land Cruiser 300, Land Cruiser Prado, RAV4 and bZ4X manufactured and sold between 2022 and 2025.
Image:
Supplied
The affected vehicles are: Toyota Crown, Land Cruiser 300 (LC300), Land Cruiser Prado, RAV4, and bZ4X, which were made available for sale between 2022 and 2025.
The affected Lexus vehicles are the ES, GX, LC500, LX500/700, LX600/500d, NX, RX and RZ. These were sold between 2021 and 2025.
The recalled vehicles are equipped with a Parking Assist Electronic Control Unit (ECU), which forms part of the Panoramic View Monitor (PVM) system that displays the rear-view camera image.
“The supplier identified that the software in the Parking Assist ECU may cause the rear-view image to briefly freeze when reverse gear is selected shortly after the vehicle is started. In some instances, the rear-view image may fail to display entirely,” Ntaba said.
The Lexus ES is part of a number of models being recalled.
Image:
File
As a result, drivers may not have a clear view of the area behind the vehicle, increasing the risk of a collision or accident during reversing.
“Consumers who own the affected vehicles are urged to take their vehicles to any authorised Toyota or Lexus dealership for inspection. A necessary software update or repair will be carried out at no cost to the consumer,” Ntaba said.

news_title: Land Rover recalls Defender and Discovery models in South Africa - TimesLIVE
efet_title: Land Rover recalls Defender and Discovery models in South Africa - TimesLIVE
efet_body:
The National Consumer Commission has informed consumers of a product recall affecting a small number of Land Rover Defender and Discovery vehicles, as notified by Jaguar Land Rover.
The recall affects three vehicles sold in January 2026.
According to the supplier, a concern has been identified where the fuel tank wall thickness may be below design specification near the inlet check valve. A fuel tank with a thinner-than-designed wall may not perform adequately in the event of a crash, resulting in an increased risk of fuel spillage and potentially a fire.
Affected consumers have been contacted and are advised to proceed to an authorised Jaguar Land Rover dealership for inspection and replacement of the fuel tank at no cost.
